article_items.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO | TechCrunch
article_items.description: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.
article_items.excerpt: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.
article_items.summary: He co-authored the seminal 2017 paper “Attention Is All You Need,” which introduced the Transformer architecture. Late last week, President Donald Trump ordered an export control ban on Anthropic’s latest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5 , leading to the AI firm being forced to take the models down entirely to avoid noncompliance.

SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future.
Shazeer, a co-lead at Gemini and the founder of AI role-playing startup Character AI, announced his departure on Wednesday .
Two years ago, Google re-hired Shazeer in a $2.7 billion deal that gave the tech giant access to the startup’s technology.
Rebecca Bellan is a senior reporter at TechCrunch where she covers the business, policy, and emerging trends shaping artificial intelligence.
The move is the latest in a series of shufflings between the top AI labs, including Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta.
In the meantime, OpenAI is also shoring up its policy credentials by bringing Ball to the team.

The “small, high-agency team” will focus on “matters pertaining to: catastrophic risk, recursive self-improvement, labor market impact, and the relationship between the frontier labs, governments (particularly the U.S.
Ball’s decision to join OpenAI - arguably an AI favorite in the administration - comes as Anthropic battles once again with the U.S.
“I am pleased and honored to announce that, on July 6, I’ll be joining OpenAI as leader of a new team called Strategic Futures,” Ball wrote on X on Thursday .
Her work has also appeared in Forbes, Bloomberg, The Atlantic, The Daily Beast, and other publications.

For policymakers, utilities and technology partners, the message is clear: This is a pro-growth, pro-affordability and pro-reliability policy.

They build on the successes of communities across North Dakota, Mississippi, Louisiana and Virginia to create a national on-ramp, enabling every region to compete for and benefit from the next wave of industrial and.

In parallel with FERC’s action, NVIDIA and Emerald AI are already working with partners across the ecosystem to build a new class of AI factories - designed from the ground up as flexible grid assets.
It underpins the technologies shaping the next generation of American competitiveness.
PG&E has forecast that, under the right conditions, each new 1 gigawatt of data center load could reduce electric rates by 1-2% by spreading fixed grid costs over more usage.
North Dakota , after adding 23 data centers, saw the nation’s largest decrease in electricity prices.
FERC’s actions do more than modernize the grid interconnection queue - the approval process power developers must complete to safely connect new energy generation to the electrical grid.
This is what the future of large-load interconnection looks like: not a burden on the grid, but a backbone of reliability and efficiency.

article_items.title: Waymo recalls over 3,800 robotaxis that might drive onto closed freeways - Engadget
article_items.description: Waymo is recalling over 3,800 of its self-driving taxis due to a software issue that could cause them to enter closed freeway construction zones at speed.
article_items.excerpt: Waymo is recalling over 3,800 of its self-driving taxis due to a software issue that could cause them to enter closed freeway construction zones at speed.
article_items.summary: The company released a video at the time showing how its vehicle would navigate freeways. Waymo's Jaguar I-Pace robotaxis were first allowed to hit freeways in Phoenix in 2024, first with employees then paying customers. The company is reportedly working on a fix and has "restricted freeway driving," the NHTSA safety notice states. Waymo recalls over 3,800 robotaxis that might drive onto closed freeways The company is working on a fix and has restricted freeway driving. Waymo is recalling over 3,800 of its self-driving taxis due to a software issue that could cause them to enter closed freeway construction zones at speed, according to a National Highway Traffic Safety Admininstration (NHTSA) bulletin seen by Reuters .

Waymo's Jaguar I-Pace robotaxis were first allowed to hit freeways in Phoenix in 2024, first with employees then paying customers.
The company is reportedly working on a fix and has "restricted freeway driving," the NHTSA safety notice states.
Waymo recalls over 3,800 robotaxis that might drive onto closed freeways The company is working on a fix and has restricted freeway driving.

Waymo is recalling over 3,800 of its self-driving taxis due to a software issue that could cause them to enter closed freeway construction zones at speed, according to a National Highway Traffic Safety Admininstration.
On its safety impact page , Waymo says its vehicles have been involved in 92 percent fewer "serious injury or worse" crashes compared to human drivers and 92 percent fewer pedestrian crashes.

Prior to that, the fleet was recalled for a particularly dangerous situation when some of its robotaxis were seen failing to stop for school buses that had their stop signs and flashing lights deployed.
This is the second recall for the Alphabet-owned robotaxi company in a bit more than a month.
Fortunately, no one was injured as the taxi was unoccupied, but the vehicle was swept away by the flood.
Prior to that, the cars could only run on highways with a safety driver aboard.
It's not known if Waymo had an incident that prompted the recall.
Back in May, Waymo recalled 3,791 robotaxis after a vehicle drove onto a flood road in San Antonio.
